Firecrawl map found the Hebrew AI in Academia guide, a Hebrew AI tools in academia page, and the English parallel guide.

Direct checks confirmed www.bgu.ac.il is publicly reachable and its robots.txt allows crawling while disallowing selected search/favorites/print paths. in.bgu.ac.il timed out for a direct HEAD/robots check and was not used as claim evidence.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery did not verify a central binding university-wide generative-AI policy page. The strongest official evidence found is BGU Library guidance on AI in academia, AI tools for academic use, and academic integrity. Claims are limited to that library guidance and do not convert it into a formal central policy.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: שקיפות: קחו בחשבון שלא כל התחומים, מטלות, קורסים או מרצים יהיו פתוחים לשימוש בכלי בינה מלאכותית. האחראיות מוטלת על הסטודנט.ית לוודא מבעוד מועד מול המרצה מה השימוש המותר בכלים אלו (אם בכלל).
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Transparency: not every field, task, course, or instructor will be open to AI tools; students are responsible for checking in advance with the instructor what use is permitted, if any.

artifacts.title: Ben-Gurion University of The Negev AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found BGU Library AI in Academia guidance and BGU Library academic-integrity guidance. No central binding university-wide generative-AI policy page was verified in this worker. Claims are therefore staged narrowly around instructor/course permission checks, verifying AI outputs, privacy cautions, selected AI tools for personal academic/research use, and general academic attribution duties.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The staged claims are based on official BGU Library pages and do not establish a central binding BGU generative-AI policy.
The in.bgu.ac.il domain timed out during direct checks in this worker and was not used as evidence.
The official BGU AI study-program page was rejected for policy extraction because it is about programs, research, institutes, and training rather than AI-use rules.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
